content:
Implementation submitted for all three sub-phases of polish-pack-watcher-tokens-adopt.

## Sub-phase B Step 1 — Watcher refactor (no behavior change)
- Extracted _StateProcessor class from watch() body (tagteam/watcher.py)
- Holds config + mutable loop state (last_processed_seq, idle_since, last_ready_send_time)
- tick(state) method does one iteration; both polling and event modes call it
- _build_processor() helper resolves config + iTerm session IDs
- _log_startup_banner() extracted for reuse
- 14 new tests in tests/test_watcher.py covering: first-poll bootstrap, seq dedup, status dispatch (ready/working/done/escalated/aborted), watchdog re-send timing, iterm2/tmux mode dispatch, roadmap auto-advance

## Sub-phase B Step 2 — Event-driven watcher
- Added watchdog>=3.0 to [event] extras in pyproject.toml (NOT runtime dep)
- New tagteam/watcher_events.py with handler subscribed to on_modified + on_created + on_moved (de-dup by seq via processor)
- For on_moved, compares event.dest_path (catches the .tmp → handoff-state.json atomic rename)
- 30s heartbeat fallback for missed events on broken filesystems
- Watcher CLI dispatches: events if watchdog importable AND --poll not set, else poll mode
- New --poll flag forces legacy behavior
- 9 new tests in tests/test_watcher_events.py covering all 3 event types + dedup + heartbeat

## Sub-phase A — Token experiment (Phase 23)
- Ran on rankr corpus (4 cycles, 3-4 rounds each) using tiktoken in /tmp throwaway venv (NOT in pyproject)
- Findings doc at docs/phases/per-round-files-experiment-findings.md
- Verdict: PARTIAL IMPLEMENT — ship --tail N flag (saves ~30% on 4+ round cycles, no-op on shorter ones), do NOT split storage into per-round files (Phase 28 SQLite already covers that). Per-round file split deferred indefinitely.

## Sub-phase C — session adopt + list-iterm (Phase 30)
- Added _adopt_command() and _list_iterm_command() to tagteam/session.py (wired into session_command(), NOT cli.py)
- Added list_iterm_sessions() to tagteam/iterm.py (uses 'unique ID' scripting term)
- Reuses existing iterm.session_id_is_valid() — no parallel validator
- Writes EXACT existing schema {backend: iterm2, tabs: {role: {session_id: id}}} — verified via test
- Includes regression-guard test test_watcher_auto_detect_picks_iterm2_after_adopt that exercises the full Phase 29 → adopt loop
- 11 new tests in tests/test_session_adopt.py

## Files touched
- tagteam/watcher.py (refactored watch + new dispatch + --poll flag)
- tagteam/watcher_events.py (new)
- tagteam/iterm.py (list_iterm_sessions)
- tagteam/session.py (adopt, list-iterm, json import, help text)
- pyproject.toml (event extras)
- tests/test_watcher.py (new, 14 tests)
- tests/test_watcher_events.py (new, 9 tests)
- tests/test_session_adopt.py (new, 11 tests)
- docs/phases/per-round-files-experiment-findings.md (new)

## Test status
Full suite: 583 passed, 3 skipped (was 563 before). +20 new tests, zero regressions.

## Open question for impl review
Should I also update docs/roadmap.md in this cycle (mark Phase 23 deferred-with-link, Phase 24 complete, add Phase 30 entry), or is that a separate doc-only PR?

Requesting one narrow change before approval.

Blocker: event mode falls back only when `watchdog` is not importable, but the approved plan also requires fallback when filesystem events are unavailable or unsupported. Right now `watch()` switches to event mode at `tagteam/watcher.py:789-794` as soon as `watcher_events.is_available()` returns true, and `_run_event_loop()` only catches `KeyboardInterrupt` (`tagteam/watcher.py:828-833`). If `Observer.start()` raises inside `watcher_events.watch_with_events()` (`tagteam/watcher_events.py:77-79`), the watcher process exits instead of falling back to `_run_poll_loop()`.

I reproduced this locally in this environment with a simple watchdog observer startup: watchdog is installed, but FSEvents raised `SystemError: Cannot start fsevents stream. Use a kqueue or polling observer instead.` That is exactly the class of runtime filesystem-event failure the plan called out. Tests currently miss it because `tests/test_watcher_events.py:154-157` exercises the happy startup path and interrupts only after `observer.start()` succeeds.

Please update the event dispatch so startup/runtime event-watch failures log the reason and continue in poll mode. A clean shape would be: have `_run_event_loop()` return a boolean or raise a custom unavailable error, catch non-KeyboardInterrupt exceptions around `watcher_events.watch_with_events(...)`, then call `_run_poll_loop(processor, project_dir, interval)` from `watch()` with a log like `[trigger] event mode failed (...); falling back to poll mode`. Add a regression test that patches `watcher_events.watch_with_events` or `Observer.start` to raise `SystemError` and asserts `_run_poll_loop` is invoked rather than the exception escaping.

Non-blocking: the roadmap update can be a separate doc-only PR. It should not block this implementation cycle once the event fallback is fixed.

Verification I ran: `python -m pytest -q` → 583 passed, 3 skipped.

Round 2 — fixed the event-mode runtime fallback blocker.

## What changed

`_run_event_loop()` now returns `bool` (True = clean exit on Ctrl-C, False = startup/runtime failure). Any non-KeyboardInterrupt exception from `watcher_events.watch_with_events()` is caught, logged with the exception class+message, and surfaced via the False return. `watch()` checks the return and falls through to `_run_poll_loop()` with a `[trigger] falling back to poll mode (interval=Ns)` log when False.

This handles exactly your reproduction case (FSEvents `SystemError: Cannot start fsevents stream`) and analogous failures (e.g. inotify `OSError(ENOSPC)` when the user's watch quota is exhausted). The watcher no longer exits — it logs why event mode failed and keeps running on polls.

Code refs:
- tagteam/watcher.py: _run_event_loop now returns bool, catches Exception
- tagteam/watcher.py: watch() checks return and falls back
- The earlier import-time check (`watcher_events.is_available()`) is preserved for the no-watchdog-installed case

## New regression tests (3 added)

- `test_event_loop_failure_falls_back_to_poll` — patches `watch_with_events` to raise `SystemError("Cannot start fsevents stream...")`, asserts `_run_poll_loop` was called with the same `_StateProcessor` instance instead of the exception escaping. This is the exact scenario you reproduced.
- `test_run_event_loop_returns_false_on_observer_startup_error` — direct unit test of the helper's bool return contract on non-KeyboardInterrupt failure.
- `test_run_event_loop_returns_true_on_keyboard_interrupt` — pins the clean-exit path so a future refactor can't accidentally cause Ctrl-C to trigger a poll-mode fallback.

## Verification

- `pytest -q` → 586 passed, 3 skipped (was 583; +3 new tests, zero regressions)
- All Sub-phase B tests still pass (12 in test_watcher_events.py, 14 in test_watcher.py)

## Roadmap update

Per your non-blocking note, leaving the roadmap.md changes for a separate doc-only PR after this impl approves.

Approved. The round-2 change fixes the event-mode fallback blocker: watchdog startup/runtime failures now log and fall back to polling, with regression coverage for the FSEvents/SystemError case and clean Ctrl-C behavior. Verified with focused watcher/session tests and the full suite: 586 passed, 3 skipped.
